text: Hello and welcome to 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: [0] 大家好,欢迎收听BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil and today we're joined by Hannah from the What in the World podcast.
zh: [1] 我是尼尔,今天和我们一起做节目的是来自《What in the World》播客的汉娜。

text: Hey Neil, it's great to be back. So, Neil, do you think you're good at preparing for the future or for unexpected events?
zh: [2] 嘿,尼尔,很高兴回到节目中。尼尔,你觉得自己擅长为未来或意外事件做准备吗?

text: Mmm, reasonably good I suppose.
zh: [3] 嗯,我觉得还算不错吧。

text: Depends what kind of event we're talking about. All kinds of events.
zh: [4] 那得看是哪种事件了,各种各样的事件都算。

text: Now, I think I'm pretty organised, but I only have enough food to last me about a week.
zh: [5] 我觉得自己挺有条理的,不过食物储备大概只够我吃一个星期。

text: And today we're talking all about preppers.
zh: [6] 今天我们要聊的话题是“末日准备者”。

text: A prepper is someone who believes that disaster is likely to happen soon, and so they prepare for it.
zh: [7] “末日准备者”指的是相信灾难很快就会降临、因此提前做准备的人。

text: A prepper might learn survival skills or they might build a bunker, an underground shelter.
zh: [8] 末日准备者可能会学习生存技能,也可能会建造地堡,也就是地下避难所。

text: Yes, and recently many famous billionaires from the tech industry have made the news for building luxury bunkers.
zh: [9] 是的,最近科技行业的许多知名亿万富翁因为建造豪华地堡而登上新闻。

text: So what these bunkers need to do is to keep you safe from danger.
zh: [10] 这些地堡的功能是保护你免受危险。

text: But they're also like something out of a spy film. They're huge and they've got cool features like gyms or home cinemas.
zh: [11] 但它们也像是间谍电影里的场景,规模巨大,还配有健身房、家庭影院等酷炫设施。

text: They allow someone to live a comfortable life while staying safe from danger.
zh: [12] 它们让人在远离危险的同时,还能过上舒适的生活。

text: A very comfortable life. We're going to be talking about these luxury bunkers, the people building them and what they're so afraid of.
zh: [13] 非常舒适的生活。我们今天要聊的就是这些豪华地堡、建造它们的人,以及他们究竟在害怕什么。

text: But first, Neil, I've got a question for you. Mark Zuckerberg, the CEO of Meta, which owns Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp,
zh: [14] 不过首先,尼尔,我有个问题要问你。Meta公司(旗下拥有Facebook、Instagram和WhatsApp)的CEO马克·扎克伯格,

text: is building a property in Hawaii, which is reported to include a huge underground bunker.
zh: [15] 正在夏威夷建造一处房产,据报道其中包含一个巨大的地下地堡。

text: So Neil, how much is he reportedly spending on the project?
zh: [16] 那么尼尔,据报道他在这个项目上花了多少钱?

text: Is it a $10 million, b $50 million or c $100 million?
zh: [17] 是a. 1000万美元,b. 5000万美元,还是c. 1亿美元?

text: Well Hannah, I don't know, but he's a pretty rich guy, so I think $100 million.
zh: [18] 嗯,汉娜,我不确定,不过他挺有钱的,所以我猜是1亿美元。

text: Well I will reveal the answer at the end of the programme.
zh: [19] 好,答案我会在节目最后公布。

text: Hannah, you've been looking into these luxury bunkers, like the one Mark Zuckerberg is reported to be building on What in the World?
zh: [20] 汉娜,你一直在研究这些豪华地堡,比如据说扎克伯格正在建造的那个,是在《What in the World》节目里做的吧?

text: What have you discovered? Well, I was joined by BBC reporter Natalia Jimenez to talk about preppers.
zh: [21] 你发现了什么?我采访了BBC记者娜塔莉亚·希门尼斯,聊了聊末日准备者的话题。

text: And I started off by asking whether she had ever considered building a bunker and preparing for disaster.
zh: [22] 我一开始问她,她有没有考虑过建地堡、为灾难做准备。

text: I love this question because I've actually been thinking about it for a while.
zh: [23] 我很喜欢这个问题,因为我其实已经想了一段时间了。

text: Fun fact. Have you? You're a prepper! Yes. When you watch TV
zh: [24] 有意思。真的吗?那你就是个末日准备者啊!是的。当你看电视的时候

text: in the US, when you turn on a kind of US TV channel, What happens is that there's usually some ads about freeze -dried food.
zh: [25] ——在美国,只要打开某个美国电视频道——通常都会看到一些冻干食品的广告。

text: I'm not kidding. They pop up quite regularly and it's all about kind of the prepper mentality.
zh: [26] 我没开玩笑,这些广告经常出现,完全体现出末日准备者的心态。

text: What are you going to do when the apocalypse happens? Natalia has thought about building a bunker and getting prepared
zh: [27] 末日来临时你打算怎么办?娜塔莉亚考虑过建地堡、做准备,

text: because she sees adverts for freeze -dried food that would last a long time on TV in the US.
zh: [28] 因为她在美国电视上看到过那些能保存很久的冻干食品广告。

text: When she talks about the adverts, she says, I'm not kidding, which means I'm not joking.
zh: [29] 她在提到这些广告时说“我没开玩笑”,意思是“我不是在开玩笑”。

text: Natalia says that these adverts are part of the prepper mentality.
zh: [30] 娜塔莉亚说,这些广告正是“末日准备者心态”的体现。

text: A mentality is a person's way of thinking about things.
zh: [31] “心态”指一个人思考问题的方式。

text: So the prepper mentality would mean thinking about things like a prepper.
zh: [32] 所以“末日准备者心态”就是像末日准备者那样思考问题。

text: Part of the prepper mentality is to question what you would do if the apocalypse happens.
zh: [33] 末日准备者心态的一部分,就是会思考“如果末日真的来了,我该怎么办”。

text: An apocalypse refers to a serious event which causes destruction and change.
zh: [34] “末日”指的是造成严重破坏和巨变的重大事件。

text: So lots of people do think about preparing for bad things.
zh: [35] 所以很多人确实会考虑为坏事做准备。

text: Natalia's thought about it, And for tech billionaires like Mark Zuckerberg, they're able to spend a lot of money preparing for disaster.
zh: [36] 娜塔莉亚想过这个问题,而像马克·扎克伯格这样的科技亿万富翁,则有足够的钱为灾难做准备。

text: So do you think they know something we don't? What are they preparing for?
zh: [37] 那么你觉得他们是不是知道一些我们不知道的事?他们到底在为什么做准备?

text: Let's hear from Natalia again, speaking to me on What in the World.
zh: [38] 我们再来听听娜塔莉亚在《What in the World》节目里怎么说。

text: So the reasons include nuclear attentions, AI risk,
zh: [39] 原因包括核紧张局势、人工智能风险、

text: climate disaster, essentially any world -ending event.
zh: [40] 气候灾难,基本上就是任何可能导致世界末日的事件。

text: But secondly, it's also a mark of societal status.
zh: [41] 但其次,这也是一种社会地位的象征。

text: So no matter what happens, no matter what comes our way,
zh: [42] 所以无论发生什么,无论面对什么变化,

text: if you're a tech billionaire, you're going to be able to live through that comfortably without compromising your lifestyle,
zh: [43] 如果你是科技界的亿万富翁,你都能舒舒服服地挺过去,而不必牺牲你的生活方式,

text: without compromising the standard that you have.
zh: [44] 也不必降低你原有的生活水准。

text: So because these tech billionaires are essentially building the future,
zh: [45] 所以,既然这些科技亿万富翁本质上是在塑造未来,

text: the fact that they're so afraid of it, quote unquote, is telling within itself.
zh: [46] 那他们如此“害怕”未来这件事本身,就很能说明问题。

text: So, while tech billionaires are worried about things like climate disaster or AI,
zh: [47] 所以,尽管科技亿万富翁担心气候灾难或人工智能等问题,

text: Natalia Talia says that luxury bunkers are also a mark of social status, or status as we would say in the UK.
zh: [48] 但娜塔莉亚说,豪华地堡同时也是一种社会地位的象征,也就是英式英语里说的“status”。

text: Someone's status is the amount of respect or importance given to them by other people.
zh: [49] 一个人的“地位”指的是他人给予他的尊重和重视程度。

text: With their bunkers, tech billionaires would be able to survive without compromising their luxury lifestyle
zh: [50] 有了这些地堡,科技亿万富翁就能在生存下来的同时,不必牺牲他们奢华的生活方式,

text: – that means without lowering their standard of living.
zh: [51] 也就是说,不必降低他们的生活水准。

text: OK, so now I need to know the answer to that quiz question you gave me, Hannah.
zh: [52] 好了,现在我该知道你之前那个小测验问题的答案了,汉娜。

text: Yes, Neil. So my question was, Mark Zuckerberg, the CEO of Meta,
zh: [53] 好的,尼尔。我的问题是:Meta公司CEO马克·扎克伯格

text: is building a property in Hawaii which is said to include a huge underground bunker.
zh: [54] 正在夏威夷建造一处房产,据说其中包含一个巨大的地下地堡。

text: How much is he reported to be spending on the project? Well, I think I gave option C, which was $100 million.
zh: [55] 据报道他在这个项目上花了多少钱?我记得我选的是选项C,1亿美元。

text: You're right, Neil. The property is said to include a large underground bunker, although Zuckerberg says it's just a little shelter.
zh: [56] 你答对了,尼尔。据说这处房产确实包含一个大型地下地堡,不过扎克伯格本人表示那只是个小小的避难所。

text: OK, it's time to recap the language we've learnt during the programme,
zh: [57] 好了,现在该回顾一下我们这期节目学到的词汇了,

text: starting with prepper – someone who thinks disaster is likely to happen soon and so prepares for it.
zh: [58] 首先是prepper(末日准备者)——认为灾难很快会发生、因此提前做准备的人。

text: Kidding means joking. Your mentality is the way you think about things.
zh: [59] kidding意思是“开玩笑”。mentality(心态)指一个人思考问题的方式。

text: An apocalypse refers to a serious event which causes destruction and change.
zh: [60] apocalypse(末日、浩劫)指造成严重破坏和巨变的重大事件。

text: Your status or status in society is the amount of respect and importance other people give you.
zh: [61] status(地位)指他人给予你的尊重和重视程度。

text: And if you compromise something, like your lifestyle, you lower the standard of it.
zh: [62] 如果你compromise(牺牲、损害)某样东西,比如你的生活方式,就是在降低它的水准。

text: That's it for this episode of 6 Minute English. But Hannah, where can people find more What in the World?
zh: [63] 这就是本期《六分钟英语》的全部内容。不过汉娜,大家在哪里可以收听更多《What in the World》节目呢?

text: Well, you can listen to us wherever you get your BBC podcasts, or you can watch us on the BBC World Service YouTube channel.
zh: [64] 你可以在任何收听BBC播客的平台上收听我们的节目,也可以在BBC世界服务的YouTube频道上观看我们的节目。

text: Just search for What in the World in the Studio or click on the link in the notes for this programme.
zh: [65] 只需搜索“What in the World in the Studio”,或者点击本期节目说明中的链接即可。

text: And don't forget to test what you've learnt in this programme with the quiz on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [66] 别忘了在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上做个小测验,检验一下你在本期节目中学到的内容。

text: Goodbye for now. Goodbye. And if you're a prepper, good luck!
zh: [67] 那就先说再见啦。再见。如果你正好是个末日准备者,祝你好运!
